creator: cs@FreeBSD.org
text:
One paragraph in 4.2 of the Handbook says:

When switching from one console to the next, FreeBSD takes manages the screen output. The result is an illusion of having multiple virtual screens and keyboards that can be used to type commands for FreeBSD to run. The programs that are launched in one virtual console do not stop running when the user switches to a different virtual console.

"FreeBSD takes manages" is wrong. Please remove "takes"
